data.title: Jungle Camps India IPO on Day 1: Issue subscribed 33.58 times, retail portion booked 62x
data.description:
Jungle Camps India IPO

Jungle Camps India's maiden public issue was fully subscribed at 33.58 times on the first day of bidding, i.e. December 10, backed by retail and non-institutional investors. The offer will remain open till December 12.

The company that operates wildlife camps and hotels is planning to mobilise Rs 29.42 crore through its initial share sale of 40.86 lakh shares. The price band for the offer is Rs 68-72 per share.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Investors have bid 9.81 crore equity shares against the offer size of 29.23 lakh shares via 55,132 numbers of applications, the subscription data on the BSE showed.

Retail participants subscribed 61.92 times the portion set aside for them. Non-institutional investors also showed strong interest in the issue, picking 17.84 times the allotted quota, while qualified institutional buyers bid for 1 percent shares of their reserved portion.

Click Here To Read All IPO News

Before the issue opened for public, Jungle Camps India has raised Rs 8.38 crore from four anchor investors including Vikasa India EIF I Fund, Saint Capital Fund, and Zeta Global Funds at a price of Rs 72 per share.

The conservation-focused hospitality group, operating boutique resorts in prime wildlife and tiger reserve national parks across central India is going to spend IPO proceeds (excluding offer expenses) for the project development at Sanjay Dubri National Park, Madhya Pradesh, renovation of existing resort Pench Jungle Camp, at Pench National Park, Madhya Pradesh, the project development at Mathura Hotel Project, Mathura, and general corporate purposes.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: FIIs net buy shares worth Rs 1286 crore, DIIs net buy Rs 606 crore
data.description:
For the year so far, FIIs have net sold Rs 2.85 lakh crore, while DIIs have net bought Rs 5.75 lakh crore worth of shares.

Both FIIs and DIIs were net buyers on December 10, provisional data from NSE shows. Domestic institutional investors (DIIs) net bought shares worth Rs 606 crore, while on the other hand, foreign institutional investors (FIIs) continued to be buyers, picking up shares worth Rs 1286 crore.

During the trading session, DIIs bought Rs 11,265 crore and sold shares worth Rs 10,658.94 crore, and FIIs purchased Rs 19,937 crore in shares while offloading equities worth Rs 18,651.02 crore.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

For the year so far, FIIs have net sold Rs 2.85 lakh crore, while DIIs have net bought Rs 5.75 lakh crore worth of shares.

Market View

At close, the Sensex was up 1.59 points at 81,510.05, while the Nifty was down 8.95 points or 0.04 percent at 24,610.05.

Bharti Airtel, Adani Ports, Adani Enterprises, Dr Reddy's Laboratories and HDFC Life were among the top losers, while gainers were Bajaj Finserv, Infosys, HCL Technologies, Wipro and Shriram Finance.

Also read: Taking Stock: Sensex, Nifty flat amid volatility; IT, realty shine

On the sectoral front, power, telecom, media closed in the red, while IT, metal, PSU Bank and realty gained.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

On today’s market, Ajit Mishra – SVP, Research, Religare Broking Ltd noted that markets remained range-bound for another session, closing nearly unchanged as the recent rebound paused. “Despite the consolidation, the continued buoyancy in midcap and smallcap stocks is encouraging. Additionally, rotational participation across most sectors is sustaining the positive sentiment during this phase. Traders should maintain a stock-specific approach, focusing on long positions unless Nifty decisively breaks below the 24,300 level,” he added.

Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by investment experts on Moneycontrol.com are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.

data.title: Kernex Micro bags Rs 2,041 crore government contract for KAVACH equipment
data.description:
Kernex has stated that the KAVACH system is a Rs 35,000 crore opportunity, with 'significant' awards to continue for next 5 years.

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Railway systems and software services company Kernex Micro has bagged a major contract worth Rs 2,041 crore from the government for KAVACH equipment, the company informed stock exchanges on December 10.

The company has a market capitalisation of Rs 2,100 crore.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The work order has been issued by Chittaranjan Locomotive Works (CLW), a Railways production unit, located in Chittaranjan, West Bengal.

"...order involves the supply, installation, testing, and commissioning of 2,500 sets of On-board KAVACH equipment, in line with RDSO Specification...", said the statement by Kernex Mico Systems. The project will advance railway safety and modernization and is slated to be completed in 12 months.

Shares of Kernex Micro were locked on 5% upper circuit in the session on December 10, and are higher by 115% on a YTD basis.

The company has a production capacity of 100 KAVACH units per month, which it says it can scale up to 250 units per month. Kernex has stated that the KAVACH system is a Rs 35,000 crore opportunity, with 'significant' awards to continue for next 5 years. Centre has plans to install the anti-collison system on all locomotives, including Vande Bharats. The company has plans to diversify revenue from non-KAVACH contracts, even as it had previously projected KAVACH revenue to double in FY26.

KAVACH is a Train Collision Avoidance System (TCAS) or Automatic Train Protection System (IRATPS), and offers safety solution to prevent train collisions.

As part of the next phase of Kavach implementation, project for equipping 10,000 locomotives has been finalised, Railway Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw told the Parliament on November 27. "The funds utilised on Kavach works so far is Rs 1,547 crore. The allocation of funds during the year 2024-25 is Rs 1,112.57 crore," he added.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The Indian Railways has already floated tenders worth Rs 2,700 crore in August for the installation of thr KAVACH system, multiple officials aware of the development have told Moneycontrol.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: Toss The Coin IPO records 85x subscription on day 1, retail investors lead
data.description:
Toss The Coin IPO

The initial public offering of Toss The Coin recorded strong subscription numbers on the first day of bidding, December 10 as investors applied for 3.06 crore equity shares, higher by 84.79 times compared to the offer size of 3.61 lakh shares.

As per the subscription data on the BSE, the issue received 46,165 applications. Retail investors took the lead, bidding 156.19 times the allotted quota, followed by non-institutional investors who subscribed 44.85 times the reserved portion, while qualified institutional buyers bought 1 percent shares of the part set aside for them.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The Chennai-based marketing consulting company targets to raise Rs 9.17 crore through its book-built issue of 5.04 lakh equity shares at the upper end of price band of Rs 172-182 per share.

The public issue will close for subscription on December 12, while the trading in its equity shares will commence on the BSE Emerge effective December 17.

Click Here To Read All IPO News

Toss The Coin on December 9 has already raised Rs 2.60 crore by allocating 1.42 lakh equity shares to two anchor investors namely Next Orbit Ventures Fund, and Evergrow Capital Opportunities Fund.

The IPO funds (excluding offer expenses) will be utilised mainly for development of microservices application, opening new offices, working capital requirement, and general corporate purpose.

data.title: Mutual fund assets touch Rs 68.08 lakh crore in November; here are the key highlights
data.description:
The number of active SIP accounts also showed growth, rising to Rs 10.22 crore up from 10.12 crore accounts at the end of October.

The mutual fund industry witnessed significant growth in November, with net assets under management (AUM) increasing by around 1.22 percent to Rs 68.08 lakh crore, as against Rs 67.26 lakh crore in October.

Overall, open-ended mutual funds saw net inflows worth Rs 60,363.70 crore. This is the 45th month of positive equity inflows, starting from March 2021. November also saw a total of 18 NFOs launched with around four in the equity category against six in October.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Here are some of the key highlights from the AMFI numbers for November:

Equity funds drive inflows despite slight slowdown

Equity mutual funds saw growth despite a month-on-month decline. For the month of November, equity schemes recorded inflows of Rs 35,943 crore, down 14 percent from the previous month. Within equity, sectoral and thematic funds saw inflows of Rs 7,658 crore, while flexi-cap funds attracted Rs 5,084 crore. “Flows into equity-oriented schemes continue to grow consistently above Rs 35,000 crore, even amid volatile markets,” said Venkat Chalasani, Chief Executive, AMFI.

Himanshu Srivastava, Associate Director, Manager Research at Morningstar believes that the decline could be because of a couple of varied reasons – from positivity post elections to global tensions in November and market corrections.

“November had weak GDP numbers, high inflation, and uncertainty around RBI's interest rate policies, as India hadn’t begun rate cuts despite the US doing so. Corporate results were also poor, prompting investors to wait and watch. Despite this, Rs 35,000-Rs 36,000 crores in equity flows is still strong, though lower than October… The festival season in November may have also affected investments. Investors continue to show strong interest in equity funds, and the outlook remains positive,” said Srivastava.

Slowdown in Thematics?

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

While the number of NFO launches has decreased over the last few months, experts are of the view that thematic funds continue to attract investors though they could be adopting a cautious approach.

“It implies that investors are sort of on a wait-and-watch mode, looking for more cues or certainty. Or maybe, over the last few years, they’ve invested so much that they might not have additional funds right now. If they want to invest, they are likely to continue through SIPs or diversified equity funds rather than going for thematics,” says Srivastava.

Small caps back in favour?

In the equity fund segment, inflows into large-cap funds, which rose last month, fell 26 percent month-on-month to Rs 2,547.92 crore. The Small Cap Fund category saw 9 percent higher inflows at Rs 4,111.89 crore, while investments into the mid cap fund category gained 4.3 percent to Rs 4,883.40 crore.

Viraj Gandhi, MD and CEO at Samco MF, highlighted that while there is net new inflow of Rs 35,943 crore in the month of November in equity schemes, but total Net AUM of the category has gone down by Rs 38,566 crore led by correction in main index in the previous month.

“Historically we have seen higher correction in small cap index when there is correction in broad indexes but this time to everybody’s surprise small cap schemes fell the least in previous month and is almost close to its all-time highs in this bounce back,” he explained.

Meanwhile, Manish Mehta, National Head Sales and Marketing, Kotak MF notes that this continued demand for small cap funds could be a result of the industry advising exposure to small and mid caps schemes through the SIP/STP route. “Many schemes continue to accept flows through SIP. This has led to small cap SIP books increasing and more net flows in the month,” he said.

SIP flows continue to drive the market

During the month, Systematic Investment Plans (SIPs) saw inflows of Rs 25,319.66 crore, slightly lower than October's inflows of Rs 25,322 crore. At the end of November, the overall SIP assets under management (AUM) stood at Rs 11.79 lakh crore, representing 19.99 percent of the total equity and growth mutual fund assets (EGM).

The number of active SIP accounts also showed growth, rising to Rs 10.22 crore up from 10.12 crore accounts at the end of October. While 10 lakh new accounts were added, 39.14 lakh accounts were either matured or closed.

Shrikant Chauhan, Head of Research at Kotak Securities, notes that SIP (Systematic Investment Plan) flows have been steadily increasing month-on-month.

“Even in challenging market conditions, SIPs have minimised market downside. For instance, during heavy FII (Foreign Institutional) outflows, domestic inflows effectively balanced the market, a dynamic that was absent until recently," he said while speaking at the Kotak Securities' Market Outlook 2025 conference.

Mehta is of the view that SIP flows aided by lump sum purchase on certain market volatile days resulted in the overall net numbers coming in above Rs 30,000 crore. “It also reflects investors' mood towards mutual funds and its benefits of being an efficient vehicle for long term wealth creation,” he said.

Passive Funds and Gold continues to find takers

On Gold, Srivastava added that the category continues to glitter with investors showing their affinity towards gold in November as well. Gold ETF category saw net inflow of Rs 1,257 crore for the month.

“However, while the fund mobilised was less compared to October, redemption number was higher. This signifies that investors would have chosen to book profits with gold trading at elevated levels and impending marriage season, where the demand for physical gold is high,” he said.

In passive mutual funds, there has been a marginal growth of 0.8 percent during the month with inflow of Rs 7,061 crore during the month of November.

Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by investment experts on Moneycontrol.com are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: SEBI proposes easing norms for NRIs in FnO segment, suggests using PAN as unique identifier
data.description:
SEBI has also proposed doing away with the restriction that NRIs associate only with one clearing member.

The market regulator has proposed an ease-of-doing business measure to help non-resident Indians (NRIs) trading in derivatives market.

In a consultation paper released on December 10, the Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) has suggested that the requirements of NRIs having a custodial participant (CP) code for trading in the F&O segment and to have to deal with only one clearing member (CM) be done away with.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Public comments need to be sent in by December 31.

As of now, an NRI who wants to trade in derivatives needs to obtain a CP Code from the clearing corporation through a CM. Also, the NRI is allowed to deal with only one CM.

These have been put in place to monitor position limits of NRIs effectivly. SEBI has position limits and monitoring of position limits for allowing NRIs to trade in exchange traded derivatives contracts.

But PAN now being introduced as a unique identifier in the securities market and used for monitoring client level positions, it could also be used for monitoring the position limits of NRIs, according to the consultation paper.

The paper said, "CCs may monitor position limits of NRIs using PAN in the same manner as is already being done for monitoring client level positions. Accordingly, no separate CP Code would be required, if PAN is used for this purpose."

The paper added that, to bring ease of investment for NRIs to trade in derivatives and simplify procedure of monitoring NRI position limits, it is proposed to issue a circular on “Operational Efficiency in Monitoring of NRI Position Limits in Exchange Traded Derivatives Contracts - Ease of Doing Investment” to remove the requirement for NRIs to have a CP Code and deal with only one CM.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: Dhanlaxmi Crop Science IPO sees 62x subscription on day 2
data.description:
Dhanlaxmi Crop Science IPO

The initial share sale of Dhanlaxmi Crop Science remained strongly on investors' radar, getting subscribed 61.71 times on December 10, the second day of bidding. The offer will close on December 11.

The seeds company launched its maiden public issue on December 9 to raise Rs 23.8 crore at the upper end of price band of Rs 52-55 per share. The IPO consists of an entirely fresh issue of 43.28 lakh shares.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Investors subscribed for 19.56 crore equity shares against the offer size of 31.7 lakh shares through 80,726 applications, the NSE subscription data showed.

Retail investors remained aggressive in bidding, buying 105.86 times the part set aside for them, while non-institutional investors picked 68.58 times the allotted quota. In comparison to retail and non-institutional investors, the response was tepid from qualified institutional buyers who bought 1.16 times the reserved portion.

Click Here To Read All IPO News

Incorporated in 2005, Gujarat-based Dhanlaxmi Crop Science with operations in 5 states produces and sells 24 different field crops and vegetables. The IPO money will be utilised for working capital requirements, offer expenses, and general corporate purposes.

Its product portfolio includes field crop and vegetable seeds for cotton, wheat, bajra, maize, gram, soyabean, mustard, groundnut, guar, castor, sesame, and coriander. Cotton seeds contributed the maximum to its revenue at 95.6 percent in first half of FY25 and 76.8 percent in FY24.

In the grey market, Dhanlaxmi Crop IPO shares attracted around 50 percent premium over the upper price band, the market observers said. The grey market is an unofficial market for trading in IPO shares till the listing.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: Indian rupee hits record low, bonds rise as change of guard at RBI spurs rate cut bets
data.description:
Indian rupee hits record low, bonds rise as change of guard at RBI spurs rate cut bets

The Indian rupee slipped to a record low and government bond prices rose on Tuesday, as the appointment of career bureaucrat Sanjay Malhotra as the next Reserve Bank of India (RBI) governor raised expectations of rate cuts.

The rupee fell to 84.8575 per U.S. dollar, and settled 0.1% lower at 84.8525, while the 10-year bond yield fell one basis point to 6.7073%. Yields move inversely to bond prices.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

India's five-year overnight index swap, a gauge of interest rate expectations, hit a 3-month low of 5.97% on the day.

The RBI likely stepped in to support the rupee, traders said.

Malhotra, currently the revenue secretary to the finance ministry, has been appointed as the RBI governor for three years starting Dec. 11 as the six-year term of outgoing governor Shaktikanta Das ends on Tuesday.

Malhotra has worked in financial services, power, taxation and information technology over his three-decade career.

He takes charge at a time when the Indian economy's growth has slowed while inflation remains elevated, complicating the outlook for monetary policy.

Restoring a balance between inflation and growth in India remains an important task for the RBI, Shaktikanta Das said in Mumbai on Tuesday.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

"One has to understand the turf, all perspectives and do what's the best for the economy," Malhotra said in New Delhi.

Das' exit, economists said, could add a dovish tilt to India's monetary policy committee as Das and RBI Deputy Governor Michael Patra were seen as the most hawkish members of the six-member rate-setting panel.

Patra's term concludes in mid-January and the government is looking for a replacement.

"With Malhotra's appointment, there is an expectation that there will be a tilt towards supporting growth," said Anshul Chandak, head of treasury at RBL Bank. "A February rate cut by the RBI seems more certain now."

On Dec. 6, the RBI kept its key interest rate unchanged but lowered the cash reserves that banks must hold, effectively easing monetary conditions.

Three new external members on India's rate-setting panel were replaced in October after the terms of the previous members ended.

"If the RBI adopts a growth-focused monetary policy, as suggested by recent dovish expectations, narrowing interest rate differentials could weaken carry trade inflows and hurt the rupee," a trader at a bank said.

The trader expects the rupee to decline to 86 by the end of March 2025.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: LIC raises stake in Asian Paints To 7% as Raamdeo keeps the 'Bruised Bluechip' on watchlist
data.description:
Asian Paints is part of the Sensex with a market capitalisation of Rs 2.30 lakh crore, and the shares are trading almost 30% lower on a YTD basis.

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Life Insurance Corporation of India (LIC) has increased its investment in Asian Paints to 7% through open market purchases, at an average cost of Rs 2891.25 per share this year, the company informed stock exchanges on December 10.

The development assumes significance amid a 30% fall in Asian Paints' share price this year, and veteran investor Raamdeo Agrawal keeping the stock on its buy watchlist as a 'bruised bluechip' in a wealth report.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

"...this is to inform that the Life Insurance Corporation of India has increased its shareholding in equity shares of Asian Paints from 4.79 crore to 6.72 crore ie, 5.001% to 7.010% of the paid-up capital..." said LIC. This net increase of 2% is for the period between January 1 till December 9, 2024.

Asian Paints is part of the Sensex with a market capitalisation of Rs 2.30 lakh crore, and the shares are trading almost 30% lower on a YTD basis.

Veteran investor Raamdeo Agrawal said Asian Paints is among the index bluechips that have been bruised and the industry's profitability has taken a hit due to a fight for market share among top paint companies, especially after Birla Opus' foray into the business earlier this year. Motilal Oswal in its latest wealth study had bracketed the paint major as a bruised bluechip under its watchlist. From the highs of last five years, the stock has seen a 32% drawdown.

During the September quarter earnings call, Asian Paints' CEO Amit Syngle had acknowledged that the operating margins were impacted by last years' price reductions and higher sales expenses.

CLSA had maintained an 'Underperform' rating, while Nomura had lowered the target with a 'Neutral' rating on Asian Paints, while Morgan Stanley and Jefferies too had cautious outlook on the business after the Q2FY25 results.

Grasim in its earnings call for September quarter said that the Birla Opus brand is now present in over 4,300 cities, and aims to expand this reach to 6,000 towns by the end of FY25. Graim claimed that Birla Opus' entry has affects the pricing and margins for rivals.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Asian Paints' CEO had denied any kind of disruption from the new entrants into the paints market.

The MOSL study suggests that 'Bruised Bluechips' bought near lows offer handsome returns, and the probability of permanent loss of capital is low for these names. "Assuming there is no structural decline in the companies’ fundamentals, such bruising offers a golden opportunity to build large positions in these aspirational Blue Chips," said the MOSL note.

Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by investment experts on Moneycontrol.com are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.

data.title: Optional T+0 settlement cycle shall be made available to top 500 scrips: SEBI
data.description:
Optional T+0 settlement cycle shall be made available to top 500 scrips: SEBI

Market regulator Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) on December 10 issued a circular on enhancement in scope of optional T+0 rolling settlement cycle in equity cash markets.

SEBI said optional T+0 settlement cycle shall be made available to top 500 scrips in terms of market cap as on December 31, 2024.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

All stock brokers are allowed to participate in optional T+0 settlement cycle, added SEBI.

Stock brokers are permitted to charge differential brokerage for T+0 and T+1 settlement cycles, within regulatory limit, the regulator said.

SEBI also mentioned the mechanism for block deal window in T+0 settlement cycle.

"A mechanism for Block Deal window shall be put in place by the Stock Exchanges under the optional T+0 settlement cycle. The Block Deal window under the optional T+0 settlement cycle shall be available only for the morning session during 8:45 am to 9 am in addition to the existing Block Deal windows of 8:45 am to 9 am and 2:05 pm to 2:20 pm for T+1 settlement cycle. The trades in optional T+0 block window session will be settled on T+0 settlement cycle," said SEBI.
data.author: MoneyControl
